notes:
To resolve the audio problem, follow these steps:

1) Ensure you have installed winetricks
2) Navigate to Warframe's wine prefix, which should be in ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx
3) Confirm that the output of pwd gives the full path to the prefix, including the /pfx
4) Run WINEPREFIX=$(pwd) winetricks xact
5) Run WINEPREFIX=$(pwd) winecfg and set the libraries xaudio2_0, xaudio2_1, ... , xaudio2_9 to Native.

Thanks to debianxfce for finding this workaround. https://appdb.winehq.org/objectManager.php?sClass=version&iId=29230

notes: I already had xboxdrv installed as I generally play with a controller so didnt have the issue some folks below reported in that regard. Out of the box, the game worked great except that spoken dialogue didn't play for me at all (sound effects such as weapons fire still worked, only voice dialogue did not). After perusing some of the comments here, I was able to fix it with the following (adjust path depending on install location): 0) pkill -9 -if warframe; 1) In the terminal, change PFX folder under compatdata dir for warframe - probably ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx for most folks [mine was  cd /gaming/steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx] 2) make sure you're in the pfx folder, then run: WINEPREFIX=$(pwd) winecfg 3) once the winecfg GUI comes up, Click on Libraries tab, enter "xaudio2_7" without the quotes in the textfield / dropdown where it says "New override for library" 4) Click the Add button 5) Click Apply then OK 6) Relaunch game 7) Enjoy

notes: This game does not work out of the box: there is no voices and it is crashes. You need to use winecfg utility to set up xaudio2_7. Go to the ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx (or where your game is) and open the terminal. Launch the command WINEPREFIX=$(pwd) winecfg . When GUI comes up, press Libraries, enter "xaudio2_7" without the quotes, click the Add button. Then click apply. I use the Native (Windows) option here. After this tweak the voices will work. Then you need to install xboxdrv as it says here: https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/wikis/Installation-Instructions . Personally i use yay in Arch to build from Aur, so i just installed it this way: "yay -s xboxdrv". Then you do "sudo systemctl enable xboxdrv" and "sudo systemctl start xboxdrv". After this tweaks the game works perfectly.

notes:
From my experience the game runs fine with some minor frame time inconsistencies every now and then. Something that seems to be an issue related to the X server, but that's a different discussion. The reason I'm writing this is because there seems some people are having issues with the game so I thought that I would chime in and give a helping hand.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
First thing first, when you start the game for the first time you'll probably have some frame drops and even freezes, this is completely normal and these will disappear as soon as proton has finished it's shader processing. So basically as soon as you have played once on every tilesets these huge framedrops and freezes should go away.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Get the game to work: ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Requirement: ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Have the "xboxdrv" package installed. ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Have the "steam-devices" package installed (might not be necessary). 
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Instructions: ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
On boot or before starting warframe run the following command in the terminal: 
 "sudo xboxdrv --daemon --detach" ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
This command makes warframe believe that there is an an xbox controller connected to the system, as without it warframe will just crash (I have no idea as to why). This command persists until you restart your computer and is not necessary to run if you have an xbox controller plugged into the computer.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀

notes:
Game runs 60fps @2k without any issues. Might not be smooth for the first time on a couple of matches though but once the cache is built it'll be smooth.
There are a couple of tweaks to configure though at least on my system. 

Here's how to set it up.

1.For the audio fix, from library right click Warframe and properties. Under General tab and set the launch option to: 
WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" %command% 

  2. There might be random crashes. To fix this you need xboxdrv package. This is used for the controller. Some folks suggested that plugging in your controller might solve this but well I don't have one. 

For this you need: 
If you're on Ubuntu 18.04  - 18.10 than you can use this ppa:
(Copy paste in terminal)

sudo apt-add-repository -y ppa:rael-gc/ubuntu-xboxdrv
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install ubuntu-xboxdrv

Source: https://github.com/raelgc/ubuntu_xboxdrv

Well you're on 19.04 and up than I don't think the ppa that is usable as it is Not updated for 19.04 as the time of witting this comment. 

A workaround would be to grab the package that is made for 18.10. (This works on my system at least). May not be recommend by most of the users though. 

(Ubuntu repo)
Source: http://ppa.launchpad.net/rael-gc/ubuntu-xboxdrv/ubuntu/pool/main/u/ubuntu-xboxdrv/
and I downloaded and installed the ubuntu-xboxdrv_20161201-1_all.deb 

Than use the following command: systemctl start xboxdrv
(Copy Paste in terminal)

The game should run without any issues. 

Do take note, when the game launcher is launched you might NOT see any update bar. But the game is updating. For first time give it a couple of mins. Depends on your network speed. 
 
Hope this helps !

notes: This solution is an alternative to using both either the WINEDLL override or using glorious eggroll's custom proton to get audio working. Then run this command "protontricks 230410 -q faudio". Viola, audio.

notes:
Ubuntu 18.04 instructions:    
    [ 1 ]   download custom proton Proton-4.15-GE-4  # cd ~/Downloads/ && wget   https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases/download/4.15-GE-4/Proton-4.15-GE-4.tar.gz

    [ 3 ]    create directory if it does not exist # mkdir ~/.steam/root/compatibilitytools.d/     
    [ 4 ]    copy  Proton-4.15-GE-4  # cp  -a Proton-4.15-GE-4 ~/.steam/root/compatibilitytools.d/

notes:
Runs like a charm after a few simple tweaks.

If the game crashes every 10 minutes or so make sure you run the xboxdvr service. If you're running Arch you can download it from the AUR.

If you experience any audio problems you can try the following steps:
1. Download the following repo: https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton
2. Extract the "FAudio" folder.
3. Head over to "~/your/steam/path/here/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx/drive_c" and copypaste the "FAudio" folder here
4. Inside the "FAudio" folder, run "chmod a+x wine_setup_native", then run "WINEPREFIX=~/your/steam/path/here/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx ./wine_setup_native"
5. Profit

notes: Works well for the most part but some changes required. As most have said xboxdrv, GE's Proton, and "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" %command%" launch option all needed to really get things smooth. While not really proton related I found fix for another problem that happens with my setup that took a bit of research. For those with Nvidia Gsync and Gnome the "gnome-shell-extension-disable-unredirect" package helps a lot. Using Nvidia's G-Sync and Graphics Visual Indicators often shows that it has reverted to Blit mode instead of Flip mode (G-Sync only runs in Flip mode, even if says on or enabled it does not function properly unless in Flip mode.) Often it would disable randomly and need a reboot but with the extention package installed you can restart Gnome fullscreen and re-enable flip mode on command. With proper G-Sync turned on the framerate and smoothness of the game is drastically improved.
